description:
Offered: every Spring
The 6-credit Communications Media Internship is an 18 hours per week, semester-long, pre-professional learning experience that is designed to link academic preparation with successful entry into a student’s career path. Interns must successfully complete an Internship Qualification Program in the semester immediately prior to the internship.
Prerequisite(s): Completion of all Internship Preparation requirements, senior status, and a cumulative Communications Media Grade Point Average of 2.5 or better at the time the Application for Internship is due.
Note: Internship placement is competitive and based on academic performance, faculty recommendations, and performance in the Internship Qualification Program.
prerequisites: Completion of all Internship Preparation requirements, senior status, and a cumulative Communications Media Grade Point Average of 2.5 or better at the time the Application for Internship is due.
